plainEnglish: When a passage or phrase is in a different language from the page, mark it with a lang attribute on that element (e.g., <span lang="fr">). Screen readers then switch pronunciation for that part. Proper names and words absorbed into the surrounding language are exempt.
whyItMatters: Screen readers mispronounce foreign-language phrases—like a French quote on an English page—unless that phrase is marked with its own lang attribute.
quickCheck: Find any foreign-language words or quotes and confirm each is wrapped in an element with a valid lang attribute; axe's valid-lang rule flags invalid values.
commonMistakes: Not marking inline foreign-language quotes or phrases with a lang attribute.
Using an invalid language code in a lang attribute (axe valid-lang).
Marking proper names or common loanwords that don't actually need a language change.

codeExamples.label: Foreign phrase marked with its language
Foreign phrase with no language marker
